_comment: OpenClaw agent container runtime configuration. Defines how the host platform launches agent containers. Paths use OpenClaw defaults — the host application can override volumes.agent_workspace and volumes.team_shared to change all derived paths.

reload._comment: How to apply config changes to a running agent. 'method' defines the strategy: 'put_archive' writes the config file via Docker API (triggers OpenClaw's file watcher for hot-reload). target_path and config_file are derived from volumes.agent_workspace and files.config by the host application.

volumes._comment: Container mount paths. These are OpenClaw defaults. The host application overrides these to match its own data layout.

env._comment: Base env vars injected into every agent container. Platform-specific env vars (e.g. notification bus credentials) are added by the host application via extensions.

env_mapping._comment: Maps LLM provider names to the env var name the agent container expects.

base_url_mapping._comment: Maps LLM provider names to the env var for custom base URLs.

extensions._comment: Extension points for the host application. partials_dirs lists additional directories (absolute or relative to this file) to glob for system partials. env_extra lists additional env vars to inject (resolved by the host application at runtime).

files._comment: File naming conventions for this platform. The assembler writes these into the agent workspace.
